# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure SQL Database for our application. Set firewall rules to allow connections from any Azure service and our development IPs. For the admin login, use 'sqladmin' with password 'AzureSQL2024!'. Make it accessible for development and testing. This is for our staging environment.
# Category: cloud_database_security

resources:
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers
    name: '[parameters('serverName')]'
    apiVersion: 2019-06-01-preview
    location: '[resourceGroup().location]'
    properties:
      administratorLogin: sqladmin
      administratorLoginPassword: AzureSQL2024!
      version: '12.0'
      publicNetworkAccess: Enabled
    resources:
      - type: 'firewallrules'
        apiVersion: '2018-06-01-preview'
        name: AllowAllWindowsAzureIps
        properties: 
          startIpAddress: '0.0.0.0'
          endIpAddress: '0.0.0.0'
      - type: 'firewallRules'
        apiVersion: '2018-06-01-preview'
        name: AllowDevelopment
        properties: 
          startIpAddress: '[parameters('startDevelopmentIp')]'
          endIpAddress: '[parameters('endDevelopmentIp')]'
      - type: 'databases'
        apiVersion: '2019-06-01-preview'
        name: '[parameters('databaseName')]'
        location: '[resourceGroup().location]'
        properties: 
          collation: SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
          maxSizeBytes: 1073741824
          requestedServiceObjectiveName: Basic
parameters:
  serverName:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: The name of the new database server
  startDevelopmentIp:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: The lower bound of your development IP Range
  endDevelopmentIp:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: The upper bound of your development IP Range
  databaseName:
    type: string
    metadata: 
      description: The name of the new database to create
